*** 2 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG FM2685 AND POMEGRANATE RD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ALONG A 21-MILE PATH BETWEEN GILMER AND ORE CITY IN UPSHUR COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS OBSERVED TO BE A WIDE AS 0.4 MILES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ACROSS LAKE O THE PINES AND HAS NOT BEEN SURVEYED YET IN MARION AND CASS COUNTIES.

*** 7 INJ *** AMENDED TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 NW UNION GROVE. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG FM2685 AND POMEGRANATE RD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ALONG A 40 MILE PATH BETWEEN GILMER AND ORE CITY IN UPSHUR COUNTY ONTO JUST WEST OF LINDEN, TEXAS IN CASS COUNTY. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO MANY DWELLINGS ALONG THE STORMS PATH TO THE SOUTH AND EAST OF GILMER, TEXAS. THE TORNADO WAS OBSERVED TO BE A WIDE AS 700 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT. ON THE UPSHUR COUNTY SIDE OF LAKE O THE PINES, THE TORNADO RIPPED THE ROOF OFF OF A BAIT SHOP BEFORE THROWING MULTIPLE RVS AND A MOTOR HOME INTO THE LAKE WITH FOUR OCCUPANTS INSIDE THAT WERE RESCUED FROM THE LAKE. ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF THE LAKE, THE TORNADO CONTINUED INTO EXTREME NORTHWEST MARION COUNTY AND EXTREME SOUTHEAST MORRIS COUNTY BEFORE CONTINUING THROUGH MUCH OF SOUTHWEST CASS COUNTY WHERE THE STORM CONTINUED TO UPROOT AND/OR SNAP NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO FINALLY LIFTED WEST OF LINDEN, TEXAS AFTER IT HAD CROSSED TX-11 AND TX-130.

*** 1 INJ *** MCCURTAIN COUNTY SHERRIFF REPORTS HOUSE DESTROYED.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 ESE PICKENS. A DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EXTREME NORTHWESTERN MCCURTAIN COUNTY NEAR THE PICKENS COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN OFF OF BATTIEST PICKENS ROAD WHERE IT COMPLETELY DESTROYED A CHICKEN HOUSE, REMOVED LARGE SECTIONS OF A ROOF OF A HOME, AND SNAPPED MULTIPLE HARDWOOD TREES. AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON, IT HIT A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME THAT WAS TIED DOWN WITH STRAPS WHICH WERE SNAPPED AND HAD FOOTINGS PULLED OUT OF THE GROUND. THE MOBILE HOME VAULTED TO THE NW AND LED TO THE FATALITY OF A MALE. A VEHICLE WAS ALSO ROLLED AT THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON TO UPROOT AND SNAP HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES AS IT CROSSED MOUNT ZION CHURCH RD, GLOVER ROAD, AND CEDARWOOD LANE. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR COLEMAN ROAD.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 6 N BATTIEST. THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED IN HEAVILY WOODED TERRAIN IN FAR NORTHERN MCCURTAIN COUNTY NEAR SHORT MOUNTAIN, WILDHORSE MOUNTAIN, AND SPRING MOUNTAIN. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AS IT CROSSED DOG CREEK NORTHEAST INTO SOUTHERN LE FLORE COUNTY SOUTHEAST OF HONOBIA. THIS IS PART OF AN OVERALL 10.2 MILE TOTAL PATH BETWEEN NWS SHREVEPORT AND NWS TULSA AREAS.

*** 13 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 3 NNW BOGATA. AN EF-4 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 170 MPH INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE FULBRIGHT COMMUNITY NORTHWEST OF BOGOTA, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ACROSS WESTERN AND NORTHERN RED RIVER COUNTY SEVERAL MILES WEST AND NORTH OF CLARKSVILLE. THE DAMAGE CONSISTED OF THOUSANDS OF SNAPPED TREES ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO, WITH SOME DEBARKING OF TREES NOTED, AND MULTIPLE SINGLE FAMILY HOMES SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. THE EF-4 RATING WAS GIVEN BASED ON THE TOTAL DESTRUCTION TO A SINGLE FAMILY HOME JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 37 NORTH OF CLARKSVILLE, WHERE THE HOME WAS WIPED OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION WITH LARGE PORTIONS OF THE WALLS AND ROOF CARRIED SEVERAL HUNDRED YARDS TO THE NORTHEAST. THE TORNADO REACHED ITS WIDEST POINT NORTHEAST OF THE EF-4 DAMAGE AND THE RED RIVER, BEFORE CROSSING THE RIVER INTO MCCURTAIN COUNTY OKLAHOMA. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST TO THE SOUTH AND SOUTHEAST SIDE OF IDABEL, WITH THE IDABEL OKLAHOMA MESONET STATION RECORDING A 108 MPH WIND GUST. MULTIPLE HOMES AND BUSINESSES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN AND NEAR IDABEL, WITH ADDITIONAL TREES DEBARKED AT THE IDABEL COUNTRY CLUB. THE KIAMICHI MEDICAL CENTER ALSO SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WHEN THE FACADE WAS TORN FROM THE BUILDING. A STRONGER POCKET OF EF-3 WINDS COMPLETELY DESTROYED THE DONUT CROSSING AND EXPRESS TIRES STORES, AS WELL AS THE TRINITY BAPTIST CHURCH, WHERE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE NEAR 155 MPH WITH THE TOTAL DESTRUCTION OF THE METAL BUILDING SYSTEM SURROUNDED BY A BRICK WALL THAT COLLAPSED WITH CONCRETE ANCHORS PULLED FROM THE GROUND. THE TORNADO CONTINUED FARTHER NORTHEAST ACROSS THE LITTLE RIVER WILDLIFE REFUGE TO JUST WEST OF EAGLETOWN, WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO FINALLY LIFTED NORTHEAST OF OLD WINSHIP ROAD SHORTLY BEFORE REACHING THE ARKANSAS BORDER. THE ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 0.6 MILES, WITH A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 58.14 MILES.

*** 1 FATAL, 8 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 W DAINGERFIELD. TORNADO MOVED ACROSS SOUTHWESTERN MORRIS COUNTY AND INTO NORTHWESTERN CASS COUNTY, TX STARTING NEAR THE CASON COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO PRODUCED EF-2 DAMAGE TO SEVERAL STRUCTURES BEFORE TRACKING NORTHEAST TO TEXAS HIGHWAY 11. A FEW MOBILE HOMES WERE DESTROYED NEAR THE START OF THE TORNADO AND THIS RESULTED IN AT LEAST SEVERAL INJURIES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRACK NORTHEAST FROM TEXAS HIGHWAY 11 TO HIGHWAY 49, DOING MOSTLY EF-1 DAMAGE FOR 3 MILES BEFORE REACHING HIGHWAY 49. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED HIGHWAY 49, TWO NEARBY HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND LARGELY SWEPT FROM THEIR FOUNDATIONS AND AT LEAST ONE INJURY RESULTED. CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 10 N MARIETTA. AN EF-3 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 140 MPH TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE SULPHUR RIVER IN SOUTHWESTERN BOWIE COUNTY, TX. THIS TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN ALONG US-67 TO THE SOUTHWEST OF SIMMS AND QUICKLY GREW IN WIDTH OF NEARLY HALF A MILE, JUST WEST OF SIMMS AND CONTINUING NORTHEAST JUST PAST FM-561. THE TORNADO PRODUCED MANY INSTANCES OF EF-2 DAMAGE TO BOTH TREES AND STRUCTURES. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED FM-561, ROOFS AND WALLS WERE REMOVED FROM WELL BUILT STRUCTURES. A SECTION OF ASPHALT ROAD WAS REMOVED FROM FM-561 AND THE ROAD DEBRIS THROWN INTO ONE RESIDENCE. AT THIS POINT WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 140 MPH. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED NORTHEAST NEARLY 4 MILES THROUGH VERY RURAL AND UNINHABITED AREAS WEST OF STATE HIGHWAY 98. THE TORNADO FINALLY MOVED BACK OVER HIGHWAY 98 AND PRODUCED ANOTHER AREA OF CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 98 AND COUNTY ROAD 1840. IN THIS AREA, SEVERAL STRUCTURES WERE LARGELY DESTROYED, INCLUDING A METAL BUILDING SYSTEM, AND THIS AGAIN GARNERED AN EF-3 RATING. AFTER THIS POINT, THE TORNADO CROSSED NORTHERN PORTIONS OF THE CITY OF NEW BOSTON, BEFORE LIFTING JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 8 NORTH OF INTERSTATE 30. AFTER THE TORNADO CROSSED I-30, NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED ACROSS THE EASTBOUND LANES, BLOCKING TRAFFIC. THE TORNADO THEN LIFTED JUST NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD 3009. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THIS TORNADO WAS 800 YARDS, WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 15.68 MILES.
